description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone with a cylindrical base, colored in teal and maroon, and a horizontal receiver bar resting on top. The receiver is connected to the base by a thin, curved cord, and features two distinct earpieces on either end, with one side shaped like a classic horn. The base is robust and rounded, with a decorative hexagonal panel on the front, possibly representing a speaker or access panel. The phone lacks a visible rotary dial or keypad, suggesting it may be a stylized or decorative representation. The materials appear to be primarily plastic or painted metal, with some metallic and rubber elements for the cord and connectors. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, making it suitable for period urban or retro-themed environments.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a teal and maroon cylindrical base, a horizontal receiver bar, and a curved cord connecting the handset to the base. The design is ornate and retro, with a decorative front panel and horn-shaped earpiece.
description_view_0: Front view showing the receiver bar, horn-shaped earpiece, and decorative hexagonal panel on the base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the receiver and the cord trailing down the back of the base.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the full length of the receiver, the cord, and the detailed front panel.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the top of the receiver and the geometric shape of the base.
